$comment: License gate configuration (scripts/license-gate.mjs, run in CI as `npm run licenses:check`). Versicle is GPL-3.0-or-later — and MUST stay so: @jofr/capacitor-media-session (GPL-3.0-or-later) and the espeak-ng payload in the shipped Piper blobs make GPL-3 the only viable license for the combined work (third-party/inventory.json#licenseFloor). allowedLicenses is therefore the GPL-3.0-compatible set actually observed in the production dependency tree plus standard GPL-compatible permissive/copyleft ids. A NEW production dependency with a license outside this list fails CI; extend the list only after checking GPL-3.0 compatibility (https://www.gnu.org/licenses/license-list.html), and record any copyleft addition in inventory.json's licenseFloor reasoning. SPDX OR-expressions pass if ANY branch is allowed; AND-expressions require ALL parts allowed.
$commentExceptions: packageExceptions maps exact 'name@version' keys to a manually-verified license for packages whose npm metadata is wrong or missing. Each entry needs a reason. Bump-resistant on purpose: a new version must be re-verified (or fixed upstream) before it passes.
